summary: CLI boundary audit confirms subjective product semantics stay in packaged Skills and agent/user review. The CLI no longer generates draft criteria from built-in templates or next-goal candidates, no longer hard-fails process-oriented field names, and its validation errors now describe objective contract/ledger structure fixes rather than judging AC wording.

sources.label: opennori-self-ac-a-12-aligned
sources.summary: Architecture Baseline alignment context. This is not Product AC evidence by itself.

sources.label: rg -n 'Rewrite ACs|from the user' src/cli src/core
sources.command: rg -n 'Rewrite ACs|from the user' src/cli src/core

sources.label: npx vitest run test/core.test.js --testNamePattern 'contract integrity validation stays structural|drafted generic goals stay blocked|high-risk agent observation|evidence health accepts custom'
sources.command: npx vitest run test/core.test.js --testNamePattern 'contract integrity validation stays structural|drafted generic goals stay blocked|high-risk agent observation|evidence health accepts custom'

reviewability: Review the listed source files and rerun the commands. The structural validator test proves process-oriented extra fields are not a CLI hard failure; draft/discover/brainstorm code still requires Skill-prepared inputs.
limitations: This evidence verifies CLI boundaries and objective tests. It does not prove every future agent will ask ideal AC questions; that remains packaged Skill behavior and user approval.
